headline: Military researcher Céline Marangé on the Ukraine war: 'What is truly in danger is the future of European liberal democracy'
description: In an interview with Le Monde, the political scientist places the conflict in Ukraine within the long history of Russian imperialism and assesses the threat that the Kremlin poses to NATO and EU countries.

headline: How China is cracking down on its Christians
Trump, with ambitions for Mars, Canada and Greenland, tries to reclaim frontier myth of pioneer America
Xi warns Trump that Taiwan could lead to 'conflict' at Beijing summit
description: Beyond the pomp as the Chinese leader welcomed his American counterpart, Xi Jinping in less effusive tones said the two sides 'should be partners and not rivals,' while highlighting the issue of self-ruled democratic Taiwan, which Beijing claims as its territory.

headline: 2026 World Cup: France reveal their 26-player squad
description: Coach Didier Deschamps on Thursday announced his squad for this summer's tournament in the United States, Canada and Mexico. Kylian Mbappé will likely captain the team despite a thigh injury sustained last month.

headline: Cannes 2026: Meeting Peter Jackson, the genius tinkerer turned blockbuster master
description: The director of 'The Lord of the Rings,' who was honored with an honorary Palme d'Or, reflects on his beginnings as a self-taught filmmaker in New Zealand.

headline: With CMA Média, shipping magnate Rodolphe Saadé expands his empire
description: 'The shipping giant at the heart of French power' (4/4). Since 2022, CMA Média, the subsidiary of shipping giant CMA CGM, has multiplied its media acquisitions from La Provence to La Tribune Dimanche, as well as BFM-TV and RMC, and is looking to strengthen its sports division. Journalists have expressed their opposition to consolidation plans and editorial interference.

headline: Lebanon and Israel meet in Washington for tough negotiations under growing pressure
Israel to sue New York Times over report on sexual abuse of Palestinian inmates
Latvian prime minister resigns over Ukrainian drones straying into country
Cuba says it is ready to consider a $100 million US aid proposal amid blackouts and protests
Slavery abolition commemorations remain source of discomfort for France's far right
What do Christians celebrate on Ascension Day?
US senators unanimously agree to suspend their own pay during government shutdowns
Warsaw recognizes same-sex marriage from other EU countries for the first time
France and Netherlands say that all hantavirus contact cases are negative
Sarkozy appeal trial: Prosecution calls for punishment 'commensurate with the gravity of the offenses'
UK minister Wes Streeting resigns, setting up possible challenge to PM Starmer
Board of Peace expresses concern over current situation in Gaza for survival of the population
'Zinédine Zidane maintains a political and social awareness that is deeply connected to his own story'
description: The sociologist Stéphane Beaud explored in an essay the football player's career, framing it within that of the descendants of Algerian immigrants. In an interview, he explains that the athlete tried to make up for the injustices suffered by his father, who remained at the bottom of the social ladder throughout his life.

headline: French far-right mayors' first steps target Europe, culture and unions
description: Le Monde examined the first decisions made by newly-elected far-right mayors. While they have not yet kept their tax promises, almost all of them have increased their own allowances, hired police officers and targeted local figures who are politically hostile to them.

headline: After the 2025 disaster, Macron hopes to make his last year as president count
description: Emmanuel Macron, who will leave office on May 13, 2027, seeks to be remembered as a president who stayed active until the end. On the world stage, he recently regained some of the credit he lost to domestic challenges.

headline: Building equal partnerships between France and African countries
'Israel's impunity in Gaza can only encourage it to be just as ruthless in Lebanon'
Suspicious transactions on oil markets: 'On Wall Street, this sense of impunity is clearly widespread'
